summary: Plan 15B is implementation-ready after Plans 15A, 05, 08A, 09, 11, and 14. It now defines one code-identity-bound policy, per-principal alias availability, memory-only private lowering, typed trust/role coverage, cache isolation, complete web enforcement, epoch-23 audit evidence, tutorial coverage, and explicit CLI separation.

review_basis.head: 07fc10b42fa0f8683630f249489368b5b072f8ff
review_basis.process: Independent reality, architecture, quality/security, systems/dependency, and final re-review against live web, catalog, Landscape, telemetry, and Filigree contracts.

warnings.detail: Epoch 23 is a deliberate pre-1.0 one-way break for populated old Landscape databases; archive/export approval and drop/recreate remain database-operator actions.
The approved required core includes web_scrape for the canonical tutorial; its existing SSRF, public-host, and operator-identity gates remain load-bearing and are not weakened by core membership.

resolved_findings.resolution: Policy and snapshot hashes bind canonical plugin code identities and safe profile-generation state.
Per-alias server/user credential availability drives principal-specific schemas, selection, and fresh execution checks without shadowing.
Lowering produces separate executable and audit-safe configs; private bindings are forbidden from all persisted/exported/log/error/telemetry surfaces.
Typed input/output control roles and a closed fail-safe trust-source model drive dominance and post-dominance coverage.
Principal-dependent HTTP responses are private/no-store with auth variation, and frontend caches invalidate by principal, snapshot, and secret mutation.
SQLite auto-stamping is reordered and PostgreSQL old-populated state is STALE; fresh epoch-23 initialization and DDL-denied runtime proof are owned.
Structured Pydantic sanitization, safe PluginId parsing, valid SemVer/hash requirements, and marker-value leak tests close config errors.
Full CLI catalog goldens and principal-specific policy-view goldens are separate.
Tutorial readiness runs the same required-control coverage validator and never auto-inserts controls.
Live DAG adds Plans 05, 11, and 14 before 15B; Plan 12 checks all 19 pre-closeout tracker steps.

verification: live catalog, plugin protocol/default metadata, settings, Landscape begin-run/config export, schema epoch, and telemetry ordering contracts inspected
live Filigree DAG has 20 total steps and gates 15B on 15A/05/08A/09/11/14 with no cycle
Plan 12 executable inventory contains 19 pre-closeout IDs and tracker metadata now says all-19 pre-closeout
Markdown fences balanced and git diff --check clean

review_history.summary: Initial review found under-bound policy evidence, unusable alias exposure, private binding persistence, incomplete trust roles, cache leakage, one-backend schema handling, weak sanitization, missing dependency edges, and tutorial coverage mismatch.
